items.text: util/mod.rs exists with pub mod frontmatter and re-exports.
util/frontmatter.rs exports a split_frontmatter() function that splits YAML frontmatter from markdown body.
split_frontmatter() handles the empty-frontmatter edge case (consecutive --- delimiters with no YAML content).
split_frontmatter() handles Windows line endings (\r\n) in both the opening and closing delimiters.
split_frontmatter() returns an error when the opening --- delimiter is missing.
split_frontmatter() returns an error when the closing --- delimiter is missing.
profile/loader.rs calls util::frontmatter::split_frontmatter() instead of its own implementation.
rules/parser.rs calls util::frontmatter::split_frontmatter() instead of its own split_front_matter().
No other split_frontmatter or split_front_matter function exists in the codebase outside util/frontmatter.rs.
skill/mod.rs exists with pub mod declarations and re-exports only.

skill/types.rs defines SkillMetadata with serde rename_all = kebab-case and all fields from D2 (name, description, when_to_use, license, compatibility, metadata, argument_hint, arguments, disable_model_invocation, user_invocable, allowed_tools, model, effort, context, agent, paths, shell, hooks).
skill/types.rs defines SkillEffort enum with Low, Medium, High, XHigh, Max variants.
skill/types.rs defines SkillContext enum with Fork variant.
skill/types.rs defines SkillShell enum with Bash and PowerShell variants.
skill/types.rs defines a StringOrList custom deserializer that accepts both a space-separated string and a YAML list.
skill/loader.rs parses SKILL.md frontmatter into SkillMetadata using util::frontmatter::split_frontmatter().
skill/loader.rs defaults name to the directory name when the name field is absent from frontmatter.
skill/loader.rs silently ignores unknown frontmatter fields.
skill/loader.rs discovers skills via <name>/SKILL.md and <name>.md patterns in search directories.
skill/loader.rs skips skills with missing or empty description and records a diagnostic.
skill/loader.rs warns via diagnostics when name does not match directory name or exceeds 64 characters, but still loads the skill.
skill/catalog.rs defines SkillCatalog with scan(), list(), get(), system_prompt_listing(), and is_empty() methods.
SkillCatalog.scan() discovers skills from an ordered list of 7 directories with first-match-wins on name collision.
SkillCatalog.system_prompt_listing() excludes skills where disable_model_invocation is true.
SkillCatalog.system_prompt_listing() concatenates description and when_to_use per skill entry.
SkillCatalog.system_prompt_listing() includes a behavioral instruction line telling the model when and how to call the skill tool.
SkillCatalog.system_prompt_listing() returns empty string when no skills exist.
Each skill where user_invocable is true is registered in the SlashCommandRegistry.
skill/template.rs implements three-stage expansion in order: backtick-bang, dollar-sign, mustache.
Stage 1 finds and executes inline !`command` patterns, replacing with trimmed stdout.
Stage 1 finds and executes ```! fenced blocks, replacing with trimmed stdout.
Stage 1 does not execute standard markdown code blocks (without the ! marker).
Stage 1 replaces failed commands with [skill shell command failed: {error}], not silent drops.
Stage 1 respects the shell frontmatter field (Bash or PowerShell) when executing commands.
Stage 1 enforces a 5-second timeout per command.
Stage 1 respects disableSkillShellExecution setting — replaces commands with policy-disabled marker.
Stage 1 runs commands from the agent's working directory (cwd), not the skill directory.
Stage 1 truncates stdout at 32KB with a truncation marker appended.
Stage 1 on failure includes the first 1KB of stderr in the failure marker.
Stage 2 resolves $ARGUMENTS to the full arguments string.
Stage 2 resolves $N and $ARGUMENTS[N] to positional arguments (0-based).
Stage 2 resolves named $name arguments from the arguments frontmatter list.
Stage 2 resolves ${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}, ${CLAUDE_EFFORT}, and ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}.
$$ escapes to a literal $ in stage 2.
Unrecognized $name references that do not match an argument or built-in are left as-is.
Stage 3 resolves {{name}} via the VariableStore using the existing expand() function.
If $ARGUMENTS does not appear in the skill body, the raw arguments string is appended as 'ARGUMENTS: <value>'.
SkillTool accepts an optional arguments parameter (string).
Arguments are parsed using shell-style quoting (double-quoted and single-quoted strings as single arguments, unquoted split on whitespace).
Named arguments from the arguments frontmatter list are mapped positionally.
SkillEffort maps to ReasoningEffort: low->Low, medium->Medium, high->High, xhigh->XHigh, max->Max.
Skill effort overrides LoopContext.reasoning_effort for the activation turn, then restores the previous value.
The allowed_tools field is parsed and stored in SkillMetadata but not enforced.
The hooks frontmatter field is parsed and stored in SkillMetadata but not acted upon.
The paths frontmatter field is parsed and stored in SkillMetadata but not enforced.
Fork mode: the agent field selects the subagent configuration; the expanded skill body becomes the task input, not the system prompt.
SkillSearchPaths is constructed and installed on the ToolContext during build_runtime.
SkillTool is registered in the tool registry during build_runtime only when the catalog is non-empty.
SkillCatalog is constructed by scanning search paths during build_runtime.
SkillCatalog is stored on the ToolContext as an Arc<SkillCatalog> extension.
The skill catalog listing is included in the base system instruction.
SkillTool activation result includes the skill directory path and a listing of bundled resources.
Skill diagnostics record: skipped (unparseable/missing description), warning (name mismatch/shadowed), info (deferred fields).
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes clean.
cargo fmt --check passes clean.
